Motivation for posts

Introduction

A) Rarely, but it happens:

I am asked to do similar things that I have already done before.

B) Very frequently, it happens for sure:

I forget how I did what I have already done before.

C) Always, it happens

I find interesting things other did and I want to do it after.

These three relevant facts motivated me to create this blog. Of course, it is also a way to show that I can do some stuff and share what I know to others.

Tags

There are three types of tags used in the categories that are on the right pannel:

Data Science Project

  • 1 Data Science tools from R for Data Science by Hadley Wickham & Garret Grolemund that defines these 5 type of tools in a Data Science Project.
    • Import
    • Tidy
    • Transform
    • Model
    • Communicate

Programming Language

  • Programming Languages used dealing mainly with geospatial data.
    • PostGIS
    • R
    • Python

ESRI Language

  • ESRI language of spatial analysis from ESRI spatial analysis books that classify this analysis into 6 categories:
    • Understanding where
    • Measuring size, shape, and distribution
    • Determining how places are related
    • Finding the best locations and paths
    • Detecting and quantifying patterns
    • Making predictions

These are then broke down into 26 different questions.

Colleagues, friends, courses or books

These posts are specifically tailored to questions from colleagues and friends or from questions written in courses and books.
